items.title: Doelpunten en overwinningen: PSV onder Peter Bosz is als een festivalletje
items.summary: Peter Bosz heeft zijn contract bij PSV met twee jaar verlengd. Daarmee heeft het plezier en het succes in Eindhoven het gewonnen van de lokroep van meer vrijheid. Hoe blij moeten PSV’ers zijn met de contractverlenging van de 62-jarige Apeldoorner?

items.title: Deadline Day: NAC-target Okita tekent in Saoedi-Arabië, wachten op nieuws bij Ajax
items.summary: Het is Deadline Day in Nederland, voor de VriendenLoterij Eredivisie en Keuken Kampioen Divisie. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers op de laatste transferdag van Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland , waar het gisteren Deadline Day was.

items.title: Farioli na eerste competitienederlaag: 'Mogen duel spelen waarin alles misgaat'
items.summary: Voor FC Porto-trainer Francesco Farioli is er weinig paniek na de eerste competitienederlaag onder zijn leiding . De koploper van de Primeira Liga speelt een ijzersterk seizoen, maar omdat concurrent Sporting Portugal goed meekomt, blijft het spannend in Portugal.

items.title: Premier League kent met dit aantal transfers rustigste Deadline Day ooit
items.summary: De Premier League heeft de rustigste winterse Deadline Day ooit achter de rug. Slechts zeven transfers werden er voltooid. En dat heeft een belangrijke oorzaak.

items.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: How's The Guvnor goes in search of hat-trick at Wolverhampton
items.summary: How's The Guvnor goes searching for a third successive win at Wolverhampton tonight - live on Sky Sports Racing.

items.title: Russell wants Verstappen title duel amid 'surprise' at Red Bull start
items.summary: George Russell says Mercedes' 2026 car is already "ticking the boxes" for the team on track but that it is "way too early" to tell whether it will prove to be a title winner.

items.title: Dit is het programma in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker
items.summary: Het geweld in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker barst dinsdagavond los. Acht teams zijn nog in de race voor de felbegeerde Dennenappel , waaronder titelhouder Go Ahead Eagles en koploper PSV. VI zet de vier kwartfinaleduels voor je op een rij.

items.title: Just seven signings - the Premier League's quiet deadline day
items.summary: A low-key transfer deadline day ended with the fewest Premier League deals completed in a winter window. BBC Sport looks at the spending that was done, and examines why there was so little activity.

items.title: Naderi joins Rangers on Deadline Day
items.summary: Rangers have signed Ryan Naderi in a £4.7m deal on Deadline Day.

items.title: Talbi’s stunning strike in Sunderland caps woeful night for ‘childish’ Burnley
items.summary: Habib Diarra and Chemsdine Talbi sat behind opposing dugouts during the acrimonious Africa Cup of Nations final last month, as Senegal defeated Morocco in Rabat. While suspension deprived Diarra of his starting place for the victors, Morocco’s Talbi was an unused substitute. But, on a freezing Wearside night, they were reunited as Sunderland teammates and duly revelled in taking their frustrations out on Burnley. Continue reading...

items.title: Benzema joins Al Hilal as Kante heads to Fener
items.summary: Karim Benzema has left Al Ittihad and signed a contract with Saudi Pro League rivals Al Hilal, the club announced Monday.

items.title: Liverpool seal $74M deal for Jacquet from Rennes
items.summary: Liverpool have confirmed the signing of defender Jérémy Jacquet from Rennes who is set to join the club in the summer.

items.title: Sources: Ronaldo dispute with PIF behind absence
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo will miss Al Nassr's Saudi Pro League match with Al Riyadh as he is unhappy with PIF's (Saudi Arabian Public Investment Fund) lack of transfer activity at the club compared to other clubs owned by the fund.

items.title: West Ham convince Chelsea defender Axel Disasi to join on loan for season
items.summary: Disasi moving out as Sarr returns to Blues from loan Liverpool seal £60m Jacquet signing for next season West Ham are bolstering their defensive options by signing Axel Disasi on loan from Chelsea. The deal for the centre-back, who has not played since last April, will run until the end of the season and does not contain an option or obligation to buy. Nuno Espírito Santo has been looking to boost his side’s hopes of avoiding relegation from the Premier League by signing a defender. Disasi was a key target, although he took some convincing to agree to move to the London Stadium. Talks over personal terms dragged on, forcing West Ham to submit a deal sheet to give themselves extra time to ensure the loan went through. They have paid a £1.7m loan fee. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Strong and smart' - have Liverpool signed 'next Van Dijk' in Jacquet?
items.summary: Jeremy Jacquet will join Liverpool for £60m from Rennes in the summer - but why are the Reds signing him?

items.title: Arteta calls Arsenal ‘most exciting team in Europe’ after Scholes’ criticism
items.summary: ‘We have the most goals, the most clean sheets’ Liam Rosenior predicts ‘physical’ Carabao Cup second leg Mikel Arteta has laughed off a suggestion from Paul Scholes that Arsenal would be the most boring team to win the Premier League, insisting his side are considered “the most exciting in Europe” in other countries. Scholes, the former Manchester United midfielder, pointed to the lack of goals from Arsenal’s front four this season and reliance on set pieces as evidence for his claim. Viktor Gyökeres is the club’s top scorer in the league with six, and Arsenal have scored 17 goals from set pieces – three more than any other club. Continue reading...
